Design (Summary): EPC-based asset indicator recognized nationally (primary energy demand plus CO2 eq emissions, or EPC class bands per Mc 001-2022).
National EPC distribution for non-residential; define worst-performer cohorts (e.g., bottom x% or EPC classes G & F).
Deadline 1: move the worst segment to at least the defined threshold (e.g., above EPC class F or better).
Deadline 2: ratchet to a higher threshold (e.g., minimum EPC E or equivalent primary-energy cap).
Further tighten consistent with 2030/2035 trajectories and Article 10 solar obligations at roof/major-works triggers.

Exemptions follow Law 372 and EPBD rules (e.g., certain heritage buildings, temporary structures) and are 'narrowly interpreted'. For buildings where full compliance is disproportionate or technically unfeasible, 'equivalence or partial compliance' may be accepted via Renovation Passports and specific measures.
